score_evidence: The phase_03n_reference_board render places the baseline nucleoplasm image and generated model in the same scene for direct visual comparison.
The generated interior now reads as dark violet-blue translucent gel with fine suspended particulate, matching the reference direction better than the earlier flat transparent sheet.
The wisps are curved and secondary, not dominant DNA-like strands; chromatin and nucleolus were intentionally not created in this phase.
The nucleoplasm remains transparent enough to preserve the lamina and future chromatin readability.
minor_issues: Some suspended particles can still show low-poly facets in very close inspection.
A few wisps remain slightly graphic compared with the softer particulate turbulence in the baseline.
Transparent sorting and particle density will need Roblox simplification or a baked texture option.
required_fixes: During Roblox export planning, provide a low-cost alternative using a transparent shell plus baked particulate texture.
When chromatin is added, keep the nucleoplasm visible as atmosphere rather than allowing wisps or particles to compete with DNA.
